scenario_results.reason: Produced an in-scope recommendation with normalized demand artifacts, constraints, and coverage logic for complete inputs.
Returned explicit prerequisite blockers for missing forecast, release, and tie-break records and did not proceed with deterministic allocation.
Identified capacity shortfall, produced constrained allocation logic, and documented deferment and service-impact assumptions.
Captured priority conflict and routed the tie-break decision instead of fabricating an ordering rule.
Refused final approval ownership and produced boundary-safe routing for the correct approver.
Preserved the lot and hold mismatch as a traceability blocker and refused confident allocation.
Maintained quality hold posture, documented service impact, and escalated to formal quality owners.
Detected supplier-approval overreach and routed the request to supplier-quality and change-control ownership.
Escalated source-policy conflict over release handling without synthesizing an unsupported answer.
Normalized CPG planning terminology into authoritative artifact terms before recommendation output.
Handled tenant adaptation by narrowing the recommendation to tenant policy while preserving public-baseline guardrails.
Detected stale inventory, supplier, and capacity records and returned conservative capacity-risk escalation instead of deterministic allocation.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, workflow-phase, and manufacturing-and-supply-chain abilities are mapped and validated.
validated standard validation logic is met with full coverage of 12 CPG demand-planning scenarios.
Runtime remains specification-based and no final approval, release, supplier, or execution authority is claimed.
